channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US has not stopped military aid to Ukraine, Zelenskyy says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Saturday the U.S. has not stopped military aid to Ukraine after newly sworn in U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced he would pause foreign aid grants for 90 days.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 South African peacekeepers killed fighting rebels in eastern Congo
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Two U.N. peacekeepers from South Africa have been killed in fighting M23 rebels in eastern Congo, a United Nations official said Saturday. The peacekeepers were killed on Friday, the U.N. official told the Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Opus Dei cardinal acknowledges Vatican sanctioned him after abuse allegation but denies wrongdoing
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — The once-powerful archbishop of Lima, Peru and the first-ever cardinal of Opus Dei acknowledged Saturday that the Vatican had imposed sanctions on him in 2019 following an allegation of sexual abuse, but he strongly denied any wrongdoing.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Auschwitz was liberated 80 years ago. The spotlight is on survivors as their numbers dwindle
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The world's focus will be on the remaining survivors of Nazi Germany's atrocities on Monday as world leaders and royalty join them for commemorations on the 80th anniversary of the liberation of Auschwitz.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas releases 4 Israeli women in latest ceasefire move
item.description: Palestinian militant group Hamas has released four Israeli female soldiers who were held in the Gaza Strip as part of the latest ceasefire agreement, according to the Israeli military. In return, the Jewish State released 70 Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Argentina's Milei rails against wokeism, transgenderism in explosive speech at Davos
item.description: Argentinian President Javier Milei called out the radical left for imposing gender ideology on children, during a controversial speech at the World Economic Forum in Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's 5 biggest moves on immigration so far
item.description: President Trump released a flurry of immigration actions this week, signing a series of orders targeting the border and ramping up enforcement. While Trump has long pledged to address illegal immigration, many of his actions targeted longstanding legal pathways.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Philippines accuses China's forces of harassing fisheries vessels in the South China Sea
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Chinese coast guard ships and a Chinese navy helicopter harassed a group of Philippine fisheries vessels conducting a scientific survey in a hotly disputed area of the South China Sea, forcing them to cancel the operation, the Philippine coast guard said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fighting in Sudan's civil war sets ablaze the country's largest oil refinery, satellite photos show
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Fighting around Sudan 's largest oil refinery set the sprawling complex ablaze, satellite data analyzed by The Associated Press on Saturday shows, sending thick, black smoke over the country's capital. Forces loyal to Sudan’s military under army chief Gen.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Democrats urge Rubio to lift foreign assistance freeze
item.description: House Democrats urged President Trump’s Secretary of State Marco Rubio to lift the foreign assistance freeze mandated by an executive order from earlier this week, cutting off most U.S-funded foreign development projects around the world.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: South Koreans turn funeral wreaths and K-pop light sticks into political protest tools
item.description: South Korean protesters are repurposing traditional flower wreaths and K-pop light sticks as political protest tools amid President Yoon Suk Yeol’s impeachment trial, with both supporters and opponents sending hundreds of wreaths to government buildings since his December martial law declaration.
item.dc:creator: Juwon Park, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Desperate families await return of 4 female soldiers held by Hamas for 15 months
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas announced Friday that it would release four female soldiers held hostage for 15 months in Gaza, as part of an exchange for dozens of Palestinian prisoners laid out in its ceasefire agreement with Israel.
item.dc:creator: SHLOMO MOR and WAAFA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombia president decrees emergency powers to restore order in coca region wracked by rebel combat
item.description: Colombia's President Gustavo Petro has issued a decree giving him emergency powers to restore order in Catatumbo, a coca-growing region wracked by a deadly turf war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli forces may not pull out of Lebanon on time, Netanyahu says
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu suggested Friday that his county's military might not withdraw all of its forces from Lebanon by this weekend's deadline set in its ceasefire with Hezbollah. There was no immediate response to Netanyahu’s statement from Lebanon or Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN suspends all trips into Houthi-held areas of Yemen over staffers being detained
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The United Nations on Friday suspended all travel into areas held by Yemen's Houthi rebels after more of their staff were detained by the rebels. The Houthis have already detained U.N. staffers, as well as individuals associated with the once-open U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japan's leader says he's preparing for tough negotiations with Trump on maintaining US presence
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, struggling to stabilize his minority government, said Friday he will seek to maintain regional security and prepare for tough negotiations with U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Freedom is bittersweet for Palestinians released from Israeli jails
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — When Dania Hanatsheh was released from an Israeli jail this week and dropped off by bus into a sea of jubilant Palestinians in Ramallah, it was an uncomfortable déjà vu.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teen who killed 3 girls at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England sentenced to over 50 years
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A teenager who stabbed three young girls to death at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England was sentenced Thursday to more than 50 years in prison for what a judge called “the most extreme, shocking and exceptionally serious crime.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump: Make products in America or face tariffs
item.description: “Come make your product in America, and we will give you among the lowest taxes of any nation on earth,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Authorities ramp up efforts to protect sea turtles as deaths on India's shores continue to mount
item.description: Dead sea turtles keep washing ashore on India’s east coast and environmentalists and authorities are trying frantically to stop the number of 600 dead from getting even higher.
item.dc:creator: Sibi Arasu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: Seven families are suing TikTok in France, accusing the platform of failing to moderate harmful content and exposing children to life-threatening material.
item.dc:creator: Tom Nouvian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 80 people killed in northeast Colombia as peace talks fail, official says
item.description: More than 80 people were killed in northeast Colombia over the weekend after the government failed to hold peace talks with the National Liberation Army.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House cheers release of two Americans freed in a swap with Taliban brokered by Biden, Qatar
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prisoner swap between the United States and Afghanistan's Taliban freed two Americans in exchange for a Taliban figure imprisoned for life in California on drug trafficking and terrorism charges, officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JON GAMBRELL and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation
